claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with the basics:

**How many children are you responsible for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 17, there are some specific rules about Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16 and over.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** For example, is Alex attending school, college, or undertaking an apprenticeship or other approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has just started an apprenticeship actually. Does that count?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. I need to clarify one important detail about Alex's apprenticeship:

**Is Alex doing an apprenticeship in England?**

This is important because the rules differ depending on which part of the UK the apprenticeship is in.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, the apprenticeship is in England. Alex is training to be an electrician here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I appreciate the detailed information about Alex's circumstances.

Based on the information you've provided, I can now make an assessment of your eligibility for Child Benefit.

According to the Child Benefit rules, you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Since Alex has just started an apprenticeship in England as an electrician, unfortunately this means you would not be eligible to receive Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time, as starting an apprenticeship in England means Child Benefit eligibility ends.

If you have any other questions about this decision or need further assistance, please let me know.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has just started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit rules, you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Therefore, you are not eligible to receive Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 17), who has just started an apprenticeship in England to train as an electrician. Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility ends when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, so the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex."]

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and has just started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit rules, you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Therefore, you are not eligible to receive Child Benefit for Alex.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility ends when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, so the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex."]
